findings.description: The CTA shape/treatment instruction (units 0010, 0012) describing the exact SVG button shape and gradient fill is not represented by any candidate rule. This is a distinct, fully-specified visual rule (shape + color treatment for CTAs) that has been dropped entirely from the cluster.

payload.description: CTAs must use the specified rounded-shape SVG treatment with gradient fill (#FFDF55 to #FAA21B) as defined in the reference asset.

findings.description: Two distinct font-role statements (Agenda for headlines/headers vs Nunito Sans for body copy in print) are merged into a single rule, losing the distinct scope (headline vs body, digital vs print) of each font assignment. These should be split into two rules or the scope facets preserved separately.

The font rule omits the governance/source facet: units 0006 and 0008 provide the canonical font resource (Google Fonts stylesheet link for Nunito Sans) which should be attached as an approved-source reference for compliance/enforcement purposes.

The CTA SVG asset (unit 0012) uses gradient stop color #FAA21B, which conflicts with the approved secondary accent color #FAA31B defined in unit 0001. This discrepancy is not flagged anywhere, and the color_hierarchy rule's hard_constraint/must polarity is not cross-validated against the CTA asset that appears to violate it.
